title: Private Prison and Criminal Justice Industry Political Spending

geo_group.2024_contributions: $3.72M (incl $1M to Trump super PAC, $775K to Congressional Leadership Fund, $500K to Senate Leadership Fund)
geo_group.revolving_door: 10 of 13 lobbyists are former government officials

corecivic.revolving_door: 6 of 10 lobbyists are former government officials

private_prison_companies.combined_trump_2024: $2.78M to Trump campaign, inaugural, and related entities
private_prison_companies.combined_state_lobbying: $5.1M (2016-2017); 87% of all private prison state lobbying

stock_movements.geo_2026: Erased all post-election gains, -60% from high

california_prop_25_2020.result: Industry won — voters repealed bail elimination law

bail_bond_industry.alec_partnership: For-profit bail teamed with ALEC to target charitable bail funds
bail_bond_industry.new_jersey_success: Pretrial jail population -20% (2015-2022); crime decreased faster than national average

police_union_spending.federal: $1.2M+ from 50+ police unions/PACs since 1994; benefiting 65%+ of current Congress
police_union_spending.reform_opposition: Staunchly opposed George Floyd Justice in Policing Act — killed in Senate over qualified immunity

da_race_spending.soros_total: $50M+ over past decade on progressive prosecutors

counter_spending.boudin_recall_sf: $7M+ pro-recall (Neighbors for Better SF: $4.8M from real estate/finance)

for_profit_probation.transparency: Almost none — firms privately held, no state requires revenue reporting
for_profit_probation.bi_incorporated: Owned by GEO Group; top 5 vendors control ~60% of market

sentencing_lobbying.cca_alec_role: Co-chaired Criminal Justice Task Force; bankrolled ALEC for years
sentencing_lobbying.reform_spending_2015: $27M by 28 groups lobbying on Sentencing Reform Act
sentencing_lobbying.koch_reform: Annual donations in 'significant six figures' to NACDL since 2004
sentencing_lobbying.famm: Koch family foundation funding + $225K from Donors Trust (2008-2014)

alec_criminal_justice.stand_your_ground: NRA brought FL law to ALEC in Aug 2005; Walmart co-chaired task force that ratified it as national model
alec_criminal_justice.2012_disbanding: After Trayvon Martin killing — Amazon, Coca-Cola, GE, Kraft, McDonald's, Walmart all cut ties
alec_criminal_justice.corporate_members: CCA and GEO Group bankrolled ALEC; corporate reps had equal votes with legislators on model legislation

immigration_detention.detention_bed_quota: Congress required 34,000 beds/day (2009-~2016); $2.9B appropriated for FY2023
immigration_detention.private_share_2023: 90.8% of ICE detainees in private facilities (up from 81% under first Trump admin)
immigration_detention.biden_paradox: EO only applied to DOJ contracts, not ICE; private share actually grew under Biden

trump_2025.emergency_no_bid: Bypassed competitive processes for CoreCivic and GEO

cannabis_and_criminal_justice.florida_2024: ~$150M spent (most expensive marijuana ballot measure ever); failed
cannabis_and_criminal_justice.opposition: Police unions, private prisons, prison guard unions, alcohol industry ($17M+ to federal candidates), PhRMA
cannabis_and_criminal_justice.2024_results: Only NE medical cannabis passed; FL, ND, SD recreational all failed

major_funders.soros_open_society: $220M over 5 years (announced July 2020) + $50M+ on DA races
major_funders.macarthur: $246M+ across 51 sites via Safety and Justice Challenge

koch_right_on_crime.texas_result: $241M in alternative sentencing instead of $2B prison expansion; 11 prisons closed; crime at lowest since 1960s; saved $4B

first_step_act_2018.coalition: ACLU + Koch Foundation + FOP + FreedomWorks + Heritage Action + Kim Kardashian + Van Jones
first_step_act_2018.provisions: Reduced mandatory minimums, expanded earned-time credits, reformed crack cocaine sentencing
